attack_story.signoff_question: Would I send this report to a company without rewriting it?
attack_story.verdict_meaning: Use the verdict as a triage priority, then read each Finding confidence and citation before acting.
what_we_can_say: The run produced only triage-level leads; read each cited tool call in the findings detail before acting.
what_we_cannot_say: Who operated the activity — this report does not assert attribution; naming an account reflects a record field, not the human behind it.
Whether the wider environment is affected — this run examined the supplied evidence only.

Undetermined: execution and persistence cannot be confirmed without disk artifacts. To resolve: parse a triage collection — $MFT, $UsnJrnl, Amcache, ShimCache, Prefetch, and Registry run keys/services/tasks.
Undetermined: command-and-control and exfiltration cannot be assessed without network data. To resolve: collect DNS, proxy, firewall, or NetFlow logs, or a PCAP.
Undetermined: logon, process-creation, and PowerShell activity cannot be reviewed without event logs. To resolve: collect the Security, Sysmon/Operational, and PowerShell/Operational logs.

claim_rules.requirement: Every Finding must cite a non-empty tool_call_id that exists in the case tool-call list.
Customer-ready reports must embed verifier replay evidence for each Finding and every replay must match the audited tool output.
A verifier rejection or replay failure must force INDETERMINATE or blocked customer release, never NO_EVIL.

Execution claims require at least two current-case artifact classes. Prefetch is preferred; Amcache, ShimCache, memory-only process evidence, YARA, Hayabusa, and malfind are not standalone execution proof.
Exfiltration claims require staging or collection evidence plus network, tool, or data-movement evidence.
NO_EVIL means no reportable Finding in the artifact classes examined. It must not imply environment-wide assurance.
Raw disk auto mode is custody-only unless mounted or extracted artifacts are supplied and parsed.
Charts, evidence cards, screenshots, and PDF exhibits support cited tool output but never create Findings or raise confidence.
Never assert actor attribution from supplied host artifacts.
Customer-visible Finding and report text must not use unqualified language that overstates scope, attribution, execution, compromise, or malware status.

release_blockers: ATT&CK coverage includes blind spots that require expert awareness.
customer release requires an effective manifest_finalize signer=sigstore (identity + transparency log); ed25519 proves integrity offline but not identity, and stub signatures are dev/offline only
explicit human expert approval is required before customer release
finalized manifest signature metadata must be present before customer release
manifest_verify must pass before customer release
expert_signoff_packet.signoff_question: Would I send this report to a company without rewriting it?

findings.description: hypothesis: Active-process enumeration failed (vol_pslist=0) while vol_psscan recovered 124 EPROCESS objects INCLUDING core OS singletons a rootkit cannot hide (core OS singletons recovered only by psscan (csrss.exe, lsass.exe, services.exe, smss.exe, system, wininit.exe); 2 duplicate System(PID 4) EPROCESS objects). Consistent with an acquisition smear / kernel-global read failure, NOT selective DKOM (T1014). Process-hiding is INDETERMINATE; a T1014 claim requires >=2 artifact classes (e.g. a carved non-Microsoft .sys driver or a YARA rootkit hit).
hypothesis: 58 processes have uncommon image names; sample: mmc.exe, mmc.exe, ManagementAgen, Microsoft.Acti, VGAuthService.. Cross-reference with disk artifacts to determine legitimacy.

indicators.note: Observed artifacts pulled from the timeline and findings; validate or corroborate before detection deployment or blocking.

analysis_constraints: Memory-only malware triage requires disk, process, network, or registry corroboration before upgrading claims.
YARA and malfind outputs are triage leads unless corroborated.
This section does not identify who operated the code or why it was present.
next_actions: Dump and hash suspicious VAD bytes before static analysis.
Scan dumped bytes with curated YARA rules.
Corroborate process ancestry, backing file path, registry persistence, and network telemetry.

next_actions.action: Collect Security, Sysmon, and PowerShell Operational EVTX and rerun EVTX/Hayabusa analysis.
next_actions.why: Current findings lack event-log corroboration for logon, process creation, and PowerShell execution hypotheses.

next_actions.action: Use read-only SIFT disk workflow to extract Prefetch, Registry, MFT, USN Journal, and YARA targets before parsing them with typed tools.
next_actions.expected_evidence: ewfmount read-only mount, Sleuth Kit file extraction, Prefetch, Amcache/ShimCache, Run keys, services, scheduled tasks, MFT/USN entries
next_actions.why: Execution and persistence claims need disk-backed corroboration; memory-only observations are not enough for final execution claims.

next_actions.action: Acquire DNS, proxy, firewall, NetFlow, or PCAP telemetry to test C2 and exfiltration hypotheses.
next_actions.expected_evidence: DNS queries, proxy URLs, firewall sessions, PCAP, Velociraptor network collection
next_actions.why: Network telemetry was not supplied or parsed in this run, so exfiltration and command-and-control coverage remains a blind spot.

next_actions.action: Close ATT&CK blind spots before making closure decisions.
next_actions.why: The coverage matrix identifies target techniques with no supporting artifact class in this run.

next_actions.action: Pivot from the first and last normalized timeline events into adjacent artifact classes.
next_actions.expected_evidence: timeline.csv plus adjacent EVTX, Prefetch, MFT, and network events
next_actions.why: Temporal clustering often exposes execution chains that a single artifact class cannot prove alone.
